title: oxalis recurva var. macrantha
intro: Oxalis recurva var. macrantha, also known as the red-flowering woodsorrel or yellow wood sorrel, is a flowering plant species in the wood sorrel family (Oxalidaceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which are attributed to the presence of oxalic acid and other compounds. The health benefits of Oxalis recurva var. macrantha include its potential to help manage conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin irritations, as well as its role in supporting the immune system and reducing oxidative stress. Herbal preparations of Oxalis recurva var. macrantha can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and health benefits.
